report_name: North Korean Hackers Escalate 'PolinRider' Supply Chain Attack on Devs

threat_actors.name: Contagious Interview
threat_actors.description: A North Korea-aligned threat actor responsible for the 'PolinRider' supply chain campaign. The group compromises open-source packages by taking over maintainer accounts to inject malicious code, aiming to steal credentials from developer environments and CI/CD pipelines.
threat_actors.goals: To compromise developer accounts and infiltrate enterprise CI/CD pipelines to steal credentials and propagate further attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors compromised legitimate open-source packages by taking over maintainer accounts and injecting malicious code into new versions, which were then pulled by unsuspecting developers.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The group used social engineering, including fake job offers, to compromise the accounts of legitimate open-source package maintainers to gain initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Unsecured Credentials
attack_patterns.description: The primary function of the malicious code is to steal sensitive credentials from the developer's environment or CI/CD pipeline, such as Kubernetes access tokens and API keys.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: Malicious code was executed via pre-install or post-install scripts when a compromised package was installed or used during a software build.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: By compromising a CI/CD pipeline, the attackers can inject their malware into the victim organization's own software, turning them into a distribution point for a wider attack.

malware.name: PolinRider Malicious Code
malware.description: Malicious code injected into open-source packages and browser extensions as part of the PolinRider campaign. Its primary function is to steal credentials like Kubernetes tokens and API keys from developer environments and CI/CD pipelines.

campaigns.name: PolinRider
campaigns.description: A sophisticated, multi-stage software supply chain campaign attributed to the North Korean group 'Contagious Interview'. The campaign targets software developers by publishing malicious packages across npm, Packagist, and Go repositories to steal credentials and infiltrate CI/CD pipelines.

identities.name: Software Developers
identities.description: The primary target group for the PolinRider campaign, compromised through social engineering and trojanized open-source packages.

identities.name: npm
identities.description: The npm (JavaScript) package registry was targeted in the PolinRider campaign, with malicious packages being published to its ecosystem.

identities.name: Packagist
identities.description: The Packagist (PHP) package repository was targeted in the PolinRider campaign, with malicious packages being published to its ecosystem.

identities.name: Go
identities.description: The Go open-source ecosystem was targeted in the PolinRider campaign, with malicious modules being published.

identities.name: Kubernetes
identities.description: The malicious code in the PolinRider campaign was designed to steal Kubernetes access tokens from compromised developer environments.

indicators.name: Suspicious Command Execution from Package Install Script
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Command Execution from Package Install Script
id: 2b1a9c8d-4e7f-4b1a-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects suspicious command patterns like 'curl | bash' often found in malicious package installation scripts (e.g., postinstall.js) as seen in the PolinRider campaign.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-04
tags:
  - attack.t1059
  - attack.t1195.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '/npm'
      - '/node'
      - '/composer'
      - '/go'
    Image|endswith:
      - '/sh'
      - '/bash'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '-c'
      - 'curl'
      - '|'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects suspicious command patterns like 'curl | bash' which may be executed by package manager install scripts, a technique used in the PolinRider campaign.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Command Execution from Package Install Script
